reasoning: Magnus Carlsen has been FIDE World No. 1 since 2011 and historically maintains a significant rating lead over his closest competitors. Furthermore, FIDE official rankings are updated only on the 1st of every month. Therefore, Carlsen's ranking on 2025-11-16 will be based on the exact same November 2025 FIDE rating list as his ranking on 2025-11-09. Since the rankings will be identical, his rank will inherently be "as high or higher" (i.e., exactly the same), making the localized probability effectively 1.00 (bounded to 0.98). For the subsequent resolution dates (2025-12-09 and 2026-02-07), encompassing the December 2025 and February 2026 FIDE lists, the probability of him remaining World No. 1 (or maintaining whatever rank he currently holds) is extremely high due to his dominant rating buffer and consistent performance, though there is a very slight element of uncertainty over months of future classical chess activity. Accordingly, the probability slightly decreases towards 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends.
